title: Anemone heldreichiana
taxonomy: Anemone heldreichiana is classified as a species within the genus Anemone, which belongs to the family Ranunculaceae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and its relationship to other species within the same genus and family. The Ranunculaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the buttercup or crowfoot family. Anemone heldreichiana is further classified within the subfamily Ranunculoideae and tribe Anemoneae. The exact classification of Anemone heldreichiana is based on ongoing research and may be subject to revision.
uses: Anemone heldreichiana is reported to have several potential medicinal uses, including its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The plant is traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Its roots are also said to have antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin infections. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy of Anemone heldreichiana for these medicinal uses. However, its traditional use suggests potential as a natural remedy for various health conditions.

parts: Anemone heldreichiana, a type of alpine anemone, is a plant that has been used in traditional medicine. preparations: Anemone heldreichiana, also known as Heldreich's anemone, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits.
